rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned in topic: the citing study asks why some ideas/new products succeed, and the cited paper is a major meta-analysis of antecedents of new product performance with a taxonomy and effect-size evidence. Given this conceptual overlap (and the title-level similarity), the cited work plausibly serves as a substantive theoretical/benchmark source rather than an incidental mention, so it should be kept for full relation judgment.
